name: Acfcu
description_short: ACFCU is a federally chartered credit union established in 1945, serving over 28,600 members in Amarillo, Texas with $331.2M in assets.
description_long:
ACFCU (Amarillo Credit Union) was established in 1945 and is headquartered in Amarillo, Texas. As a not-for-profit credit union, it operates with a community-focused mission to provide financial services to local residents and businesses. The organization stewards over $331.2 million in assets and maintains a modest physical footprint with 1 branch location and 6 ATMs, supported by over 90 dedicated employees as of 2026.

ACFCU offers a comprehensive suite of consumer banking and lending products including checking and savings accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), auto loans, personal loans, and mortgages. Members can access bill pay, online loan payments, remote deposit capture, e-statements, and mobile banking via iOS and Android apps. The credit union also provides financial counseling, financial education resources, and student scholarships. Auto loans are available for new and used vehicles, with mortgage options including fixed-rate (15+ years and 15 years or less) and adjustable-rate mortgages. Wire transfers and drive-thru teller services are available, along with a branch locator and financial calculators.

As a credit union rather than a traditional bank, ACFCU operates on a not-for-profit basis and advertises competitive rates and lower fees than for-profit banks. Their rates reflect this model: new vehicle loans at 4.05% APR, used vehicle loans at 5.69% APR, and unsecured credit cards at 0.00% introductory rates. The organization maintains service status monitoring for online banking, mobile app, and traditional services. With 28,600+ members across various account types (50,000+ in regular savings, 13,600+ in share draft accounts, 1,500+ in CDs), ACFCU demonstrates stable community reach.

ACFCU is best suited for Amarillo residents and those within their membership eligibility area seeking basic to intermediate banking services. However, the single-branch, limited-ATM footprint restricts accessibility compared to regional or national banks. The absence of customer reviews on the reference platform limits transparency regarding service quality and member satisfaction. This credit union serves a specific geographic community and is optimized for local members rather than those seeking extensive branch networks or national ATM access. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Competitive auto loan rates: new vehicles at 4.05% APR, used at 5.69% APR
Not-for-profit structure provides lower fees and better rates than for-profit banks
Comprehensive digital banking: mobile app (iOS/Android), online bill pay, remote deposit capture, e-statements
Established 1945 with $331.2M in assets and 28,600+ members demonstrates stability and longevity
Diverse loan products: auto loans, personal loans, mortgages (fixed/adjustable), and credit lines
Member benefits: financial counseling, financial education, and student scholarships
6 ATMs and drive-thru teller service for basic accessibility in Amarillo
cons: Single branch location limits physical accessibility and severely restricts in-person service convenience
Only 6 ATMs available, minimal network for cash access compared to larger institutions
Membership eligibility restricted to specific geographic community; not open to all consumers nationally
No customer reviews available on the platform, limiting visibility into actual member satisfaction and service quality
Limited mortgage product transparency; requires contacting credit union directly for specific terms and rates
best_for: Amarillo, Texas residents seeking stable, community-focused banking with competitive rates
Local members wanting lower fees and better rates than traditional for-profit banks
Borrowers interested in auto loans with rates starting at 4.05% for new vehicles
Community members desiring financial education and counseling services
diagnosis: ACFCU is best for Amarillo-area residents and those within their membership eligibility zone who value not-for-profit banking with competitive rates and comprehensive digital services. The primary caveat is the single physical branch and limited ATM network, which makes this institution unsuitable for consumers requiring extensive in-person access or those living outside Amarillo; additionally, the lack of public reviews limits transparency into actual member experience.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
